claim_text: Pedestrians receptivity toward FAVs significantly increased with the inclusion of external features
Walking silhouette and braking text were the most favored visual interfaces while verbal message was the preferred audible feature
Females and people from 30+ age group reacted most positively to the external features
Pedestrians who commit errors or show aggressive behaviors rated FAVs poorly even with external features
Pedestrians who intentionally violate traffic rules or get distracted became more cautious with FAVs and appreciated external features
